title: ProcScript: Precompiler Directives and Uniface Constants
breadcrumbs: ProcScript: Precompiler Directives and Uniface Constants
text: ProcScript: Precompiler Directives and Uniface Constants Precompiler directives and Uniface constants enable you to produce generalized, reusable ProcScript. They allow you to determine the exact composition of the ProcScript that will be compiled. Precompiler Directives #comment #define #else #elseif #enddefine #endfor #endif #entry #error #for #function #if #ifdefined #ifundefined #include #info #pragma #startdefine #undefine #warning Uniface Constants Uniface constants comprise a set of predefined constants that can be used in ProcScript to refer to objects and properties. Note: These constants should only be used in components, not in global ProcScript, because during compilation, global ProcScript does not have access to the information required to correctly resolve the constants. <$cmpStateManagedBy> <$componentName> <$componentType> <$entName> <$fieldName> <$libraryName> <$modelName> <$tableName> <$triggerAbbr> Related Links Precompiler Directives: Descriptions Precompiler directives are symbols used in ProcScript that are parsed and interpreted by the precompiler to insert or generate constants, IncludeScripts, and other code. Predefined Constants: Descriptions Uniface provides a set of predefined constants that can be used in ProcScript to refer to objects and properties, such as a field or entity. Was this topic helpful?
